records.ety: [OE. forgeten, foryeten, AS. forgietan, forgitan; pref. for- + gietan, gitan (only in comp.), to get; cf. D. vergeten, G. vergessen, Sw. förgäta, Dan. forgiette. See For-, and Get, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. Forgot (?) (Forgat (<?/), Obs.); p. p. Forgotten (?), Forgot; p. pr. & vb. n. Forgetting.]

defs: To lose the remembrance of; to let go from the memory; to cease to have in mind; not to think of; also, to lose the power of; to cease from doing.
quotes: Bless the Lord, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits.
Let my right hand forget her cunning.
Hath thy knee forget to bow?

defs: To treat with inattention or disregard; to slight; to neglect.
quotes: Can a woman forget her sucking child? . . . Yes, they may forget, yet will I not forget thee.
